What is really driving listeners?

Is it the war, the oil prices, or the podcast guest?

Welcome to my visual data story

I work on a podcast that goes live from Monday to Friday. The podcast is about global economics and politics. Number of listeners go up and down all the time and I am curious to understand more about what drives listeners.

Is the headline making people listen to the podcast?

I created two groups, one for the episodes that contain war-related words in its headlines, and the other for those that don’t. As seen in the plot below, the amount of listeners is higher in the first group.

A plot showing the relationship between the podcast’s amount of listeners and the use of war-related words in its headline.

Maybe the listeners have preferences over which economist in invited to talk.

In the podcast, there is a group of economists that are invited to talk as guests. They rotate from episode to episode, but they are always the same group. I believe that usual listeners might have preferences for some economists over the others. In the bar plot below, we can see that indeed, there are some economists with higher average listeners than others. For example “Mahjabeen” has the highest average listeners (average among her episodes).

A bar-plot showing the relationship between the podcast’s amount of listeners and the name of the guest economist.

Is every day the same? Or are there differences in the amount of listeners between the days of the week?

We can see that on Mondays, there are less listeners than on the rest of the days. This makes sense with the stock market’s time difference I mention in the project4_report.Rmd. In addition, it seems like Fridays are slightly lower than Tue, Wed, and Thu. This was unexpected to me as I cannot come up with any explanation other than maybe people are more lazy on Fridays because TGIF!

A box-plot showing the relationship between the podcast’s amount of listeners and the day of the week the podcast was aired

Does the podcast have more listeners since I have done project 2?

Well… This is a very sad reality, because it means the war has escalated. The podcast is having more listeners in May 2026 as compared to those in March 2026.

## What are the top 4 podcasts with the most listeners?

And to finish up on a nice note, these are the top 4 podcasts!! (The ones that received more listeners from both Project 2 and Project 4)
